name: Unusual Chinese Grilled Shortribs
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the shortribs into short, flat strips. Place each rib fat-side-up on a cutting board and slice about 1/4 inch high through almost, but not all, of the bottom-most section of the rib.
2. Turn the meat over and continue slicing another 1/4 inch layers until the rib is completely layered and forms one long strip.
3. Using the edge of a Chinese knife or other flat surface, pound the meat to an even thickness. Chill the meat well; this cutting method does not work well on room temperature ribs.
4. For the marinade (curry-based blender sauce):
- Blend all liquid ingredients in a blender for about 15 seconds at "Blend" speed.
- Grate fresh ginger root and combine with minced garlic.
- Mince the white portion of the green onions.
- Add these ingredients to the blender mixture and operate on "low" for about 45 seconds.
5. Rub the sauce onto butterflied ribs and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (or more).
6. Place ribs on the grill, basting often with sauce, until done. Watch carefully to determine when they are cooked through, which may take about 30 minutes depending on the heat of the grill.
7. Note: This sauce is excellent for chicken as well.
